title: Simple past — regular verbs (-ed)
description: Learn how to form and use the simple past tense with regular verbs that end in -ed.

tasks.title: Forming the simple past — regular verbs

content.explanation: Most English verbs are 'regular' — you simply add -ed to make the past tense.

rules.rule: Short verb + consonant: double the final consonant + -ed
Verb ends in consonant + y: change y → i + -ed

content.important: The past form is the SAME for all subjects — I worked, She worked, They worked.

content.explanation: For negatives and questions in simple past, use 'did' or 'didn't' + BASE verb.

forms.example: Yes, she did. / No, she didn't.

common_error.right: She didn't work. (base verb after didn't)

content.explanation: Learn these very common regular verbs and their past forms.

verbs.example: She watched a film last night.

content.instruction: The -ed ending has 3 different sounds. Listen carefully.

pronunciation_groups.note: After voiceless sounds: k, p, f, s, ch

phrases.text: I walked to work and arrived at nine.
She studied hard and finished the exam.
They visited their family and stayed for a week.

content.question: Which is the correct past tense of 'study'?
content.explanation: Consonant + y → change y to i + ed. study → studied ✓

content.question: Which is the correct past form of 'stop'?
content.explanation: Short verb ending in consonant → double consonant + ed. stop → stopped ✓

content.question: 'She ___ finish her homework last night.'
content.explanation: Negative past: didn't + BASE verb. didn't finish ✓ (never add -ed after didn't)

content.question: Make a question: 'You watched the match.' → '___ you ___ the match?'
content.explanation: Question: Did + subject + BASE verb (no -ed). Did you watch? ✓

tasks.title: Fill in the correct past form

content.sentence: Yesterday I ___ (walk) to the office, ___ (talk) to my manager, and ___ (finish) my report by noon.
content.explanation: All regular verbs in simple past: add -ed. walked, talked, finished.

content.sentence: I ___ (not cook) dinner last night — I ___ (order) food from outside. It ___ (arrive) quickly.
content.explanation: Negative: didn't + base (didn't cook). Positive: verb+ed (ordered, arrived).

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past tense sentence.
content.correct_sentence: We visited my grandparents last weekend.
content.explanation: Subject + past verb + object + time expression.

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct question.
content.explanation: Did + subject + base verb + object?

tasks.title: Match base verb to past form

content.instruction: Match each verb to its correct regular past form.
Which sentence is fully correct?
content.wrong_sentence: She didn't cooked dinner and they didn't watched the film.
options: She didn't cooked dinner and they didn't watched the film.
She didn't cook dinner and they didn't watch the film.
She didn't cook dinner and they didn't watched the film.
She didn't cooked dinner and they didn't watch the film.
content.explanation: didn't + BASE verb (never add -ed after didn't). didn't cook ✓, didn't watch ✓

tasks.title: Dialogue — What did you do yesterday?

content.context: Two friends Nisha and Arjun talk about their day over a phone call.

dialogue.speaker: Nisha
dialogue.line: Hey Arjun! What did you do yesterday?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.line: I had a really busy day! I woke up early and walked to the gym.

dialogue.speaker: Nisha
dialogue.line: Oh nice! Did you work out for long?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.line: About an hour. Then I cooked breakfast, cleaned the kitchen, and started working.

dialogue.speaker: Nisha
dialogue.line: Did you finish your project?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.line: Almost! I worked until 7pm. Then I called my parents and talked with them for a while.

dialogue.speaker: Nisha
dialogue.line: Did you go out at all?

dialogue.speaker: Arjun
dialogue.line: No, I didn't. I was really tired. I just watched a show and went to bed early.

dialogue.speaker: Nisha
dialogue.line: Sounds productive! I didn't do much — I just relaxed at home all day!

comprehension_questions.question: Where did Arjun go in the morning?
Until what time did Arjun work?
Did Arjun go out in the evening?
options: Yes, he went for a walk
Yes, he went to a restaurant
No, he went to sleep early

content.instruction: This paragraph has 3 past tense errors. Choose the corrected version.
content.wrong_text: Yesterday I walk to the park and played with my dog. After that I cook lunch and clean my room. I didn't watched TV because I studied for my exam.
options: Yesterday I walked to the park and played with my dog. After that I cooked lunch and cleaned my room. I didn't watch TV because I studied for my exam.
Yesterday I walked to the park and played with my dog. After that I cook lunch and cleaned my room. I didn't watched TV because I studied for my exam.
Yesterday I walk to the park and played with my dog. After that I cooked lunch and cleaned my room. I didn't watch TV because I studied for my exam.
Yesterday I walked to the park and played with my dog. After that I cooked lunch and cleaned my room. I didn't watched TV because I study for my exam.
content.explanation: walk→walked, cook→cooked, didn't watched→didn't watch (base after didn't).

content.scenario: Your manager asks: 'Did you finish the report I asked for?' You did finish it.
content.question: What is the most professional and natural reply?
options: Yes, I finished it this morning and sent it to you by email.
Yes, I finish it this morning.
Yes, I was finishing it morning.
content.explanation: finished (past ✓), sent (irregular past ✓), by email (natural addition).

tasks.title: Speaking — What did you do yesterday?

content.instruction: Speak for 60 seconds. Describe everything you did yesterday from morning to night. Use at least 8 different past tense verbs.
content.prompt: Tell me about your yesterday. What did you do?
content.example_response: Yesterday I woke up at 6:30. I walked to the gym and exercised for forty minutes. Then I arrived home, cooked breakfast, and cleaned my room. I worked from nine in the morning until about six in the evening. I called my friend in the afternoon and we talked for half an hour. In the evening I cooked dinner, watched a film, and then studied English for thirty minutes. I finished at about ten o'clock. I didn't go out but I had a productive day!

tasks.title: Speaking — Ask and answer past questions

content.instruction: Play both roles. Ask 4 questions about what someone did last weekend and give natural answers.
content.prompt: Have a conversation about last weekend. Ask: what did you do / where did you go / did you enjoy it / who did you talk to?
content.example_response: What did you do last weekend? — I visited my grandparents in Madurai. It was a long drive! Did you enjoy it? — Yes, I loved it! We talked, cooked together, and played cards. Did you go anywhere interesting? — We walked to the temple on Sunday morning. Did you call anyone? — I called my college friend on Saturday evening and we talked for ages!
key_phrases: Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.

what_you_learned: Spelling rules: -e verbs add -d (live→lived), -y verbs change y→i+ed (study→studied), short consonant doubles (stop→stopped)
Same form for ALL subjects: I/she/they worked
Short answers: Yes, I did. / No, she didn't.

title: Simple past — irregular verbs
description: Learn the most important irregular past tense verbs — verbs that do NOT follow the -ed pattern.

tasks.title: Irregular verbs — Group 1: completely different form

content.explanation: These very common verbs have a completely different past tense form. You must memorise them!

verbs.example: I got a message from her.

tasks.title: Irregular verbs — Group 2: more key verbs

content.explanation: More essential irregular verbs you need for everyday conversation.

verbs.example: She drank two cups of tea.

tasks.title: Key rule: didn't + BASE verb always

content.explanation: Even for irregular verbs, the negative and question use DID/DIDN'T + the BASE verb (not the past form).

examples.negative: She didn't go to the cinema.

content.rule: Positive → use irregular past (went, ate, slept). Negative/Question → didn't / did + BASE verb.

content.instruction: Repeat these sentences with irregular past verbs. Focus on the irregular forms.

phrases.text: I woke up late and had a quick breakfast.
She went to the market and bought some vegetables.
We ate dinner at a nice restaurant and drank mango juice.
He didn't sleep well — he felt tired all day.
Did you see that film? — Yes, I thought it was excellent!

content.question: What is the past tense of 'go'?
content.explanation: Go is irregular → went. I went to school. ✓

content.question: What is the past tense of 'buy'?
content.explanation: Buy is irregular → bought. She bought a dress. ✓

content.question: 'They ___ to the party last night — they were busy.'
content.explanation: Negative past: didn't + BASE verb. didn't go ✓ (never 'didn't went')

content.question: '___ she ___ the new café yesterday?'
content.explanation: Question: Did + subject + BASE verb. Did she see ✓ (not 'saw')

tasks.title: Fill in the irregular past verb

content.sentence: Yesterday I ___ (wake) up at 7, ___ (have) tea, and ___ (go) to the office by metro.
content.explanation: wake→woke, have→had, go→went. All irregular.

content.sentence: We ___ (eat) at home last night — we ___ (go) to a restaurant because it was our anniversary.
content.explanation: Negative: didn't eat (base after didn't). Positive: went (irregular past of go).

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past tense sentence with irregular verbs.
content.correct_sentence: She met her friend and they drank coffee.
content.explanation: met (meet→met), drank (drink→drank) — both irregular.

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct negative past tense sentence.
content.explanation: didn't + BASE verb. know is the base of knew → I didn't know. ✓

tasks.title: Match base verb to irregular past

content.instruction: Match each base verb to its correct irregular past form.
This sentence has 2 errors. Choose the corrected version.
content.wrong_sentence: He didn't went to work because he felt sick, so he sleeped all day.
options: He didn't went to work because he felt sick, so he sleeped all day.
He didn't go to work because he felt sick, so he slept all day.
He didn't go to work because he feeled sick, so he slept all day.
He didn't go to work because he felt sick, so he sleeped all day.
content.explanation: didn't + BASE verb (go not went). sleep→slept (irregular). felt is already correct.

tasks.title: Dialogue — A story from the past

content.context: Grandma tells young Ananya about a special memory from her youth.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: Grandma, tell me a story from when you were young!

dialogue.speaker: Grandma
dialogue.line: Okay! When I was seventeen, my friend and I went on a trip to Ooty.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: Just the two of you? Weren't you scared?

dialogue.speaker: Grandma
dialogue.line: A little! We took the train — it was a long journey. We ate snacks and sang songs all the way.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: Did you stay there long?

dialogue.speaker: Grandma
dialogue.line: Three days. We slept in a small guesthouse. We woke up early every morning and saw the sunrise in the mountains.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: That sounds amazing! Did you buy anything?

dialogue.speaker: Grandma
dialogue.line: I bought a beautiful shawl — I still have it! And we drank the most delicious tea I ever tasted.

dialogue.speaker: Ananya
dialogue.line: I want to go to Ooty too!

dialogue.speaker: Grandma
dialogue.line: You should! I thought about that trip for years. It made me so happy.

comprehension_questions.question: How old was Grandma when she went to Ooty?

content.scenario: Your friend asks: 'Did you go to Ravi's party last night?'
content.question: You went but left early. Which is the most natural reply?
options: Yes, I went but I didn't stay long — I left early because I felt tired.
Yes, I goed but I leaved early.
Yes, I went but I didn't stayed long.
Yes, I go but left before.
content.explanation: went ✓ (irregular), didn't stay ✓ (base after didn't), left ✓ (irregular past of leave), felt ✓.

tasks.title: Speaking — Tell a story from last week

content.instruction: Speak for 65 seconds. Tell a story about something you did last week. Use at least 8 irregular past verbs.
content.prompt: Tell me about something interesting you did last week. Use: went, had, ate, saw, met, bought, felt, thought, woke, took, got.
content.example_response: Last week I did something really fun. On Thursday I woke up early and took the metro to Marina Beach. I met my college friend there and we had breakfast at a small café near the beach. I ate idli and drank strong filter coffee. We saw the sunrise — it was absolutely beautiful. After that we walked along the beach for an hour. I felt so relaxed and happy. We went to a bookshop later and I bought a novel I wanted for a long time. In the evening I came home, felt tired, and slept for nine hours! I thought it was one of the best days I had in months.
key_phrases: went, had, ate, saw, met, bought, felt, thought, woke, took, got, came, slept, drank

content.instruction: Make one sentence for each irregular verb: went, ate, saw, bought, slept, met, felt, woke, drank, gave. 10 verbs = 10 sentences.
content.prompt: Use each irregular past verb in a complete sentence about your own life.
content.example_response: I went to the market on Sunday. I ate dal rice for lunch today. I saw a beautiful rainbow last week. I bought new shoes last month. I slept for only five hours last night. I met my neighbour at the bus stop this morning. I felt very proud when I finished my project. I woke up at 6am today. I drank chai three times yesterday. I gave my friend a birthday gift last week.
key_phrases: went / ate / saw / bought / slept / met / felt / woke / drank / gave

content.instruction: Choose the paragraph with all irregular verbs correct.
content.wrong_text: Last Sunday I goed to the temple with my family. We waked up at 5am and taked the bus. We seed many people there. Afterwards we eated at a hotel and drinkd fresh juice. I feeled very happy and peaceful.
options: Last Sunday I went to the temple with my family. We woke up at 5am and took the bus. We saw many people there. Afterwards we ate at a hotel and drank fresh juice. I felt very happy and peaceful.
Last Sunday I went to the temple with my family. We woke up at 5am and took the bus. We saw many people there. Afterwards we ate at a hotel and drink fresh juice. I felt very happy and peaceful.
Last Sunday I went to the temple with my family. We waked up at 5am and took the bus. We saw many people there. Afterwards we ate at a hotel and drank fresh juice. I feeled very happy and peaceful.
Last Sunday I goed to the temple with my family. We woke up at 5am and took the bus. We saw many people there. Afterwards we eaten at a hotel and drank juice. I felt very happy.
content.explanation: went, woke, took, saw, ate, drank, felt — all correct irregular past forms.

what_you_learned: Group 1 irregular: go→went, come→came, see→saw, have→had, get→got, give→gave, take→took, make→made
Group 2 irregular: eat→ate, drink→drank, sleep→slept, wake→woke, meet→met, buy→bought, feel→felt, write→wrote
Negative ALWAYS: didn't + BASE verb (not past form)
Question ALWAYS: Did + subject + BASE verb?
Positive uses irregular form, negative/question uses BASE verb

title: Past negative — didn't (practice & fluency)
description: Deep practice with past negatives — contrasting what you did and didn't do, building natural fluency.

content.explanation: Didn't (did + not) makes the negative past for ALL verbs — regular and irregular.
content.structure: Subject + didn't + BASE verb + rest of sentence

examples.negative: I didn't go to the gym.

contrast_pattern.explanation: Very useful: say what you DID and what you DIDN'T do in the same sentence.
examples: I went to work but I didn't finish my report.
She cooked dinner but she didn't wash the dishes.
We went to the market but we didn't buy anything.

content.explanation: When you say you DIDN'T do something, give a reason with 'because'.
examples: I didn't go to the party because I was tired.
She didn't eat lunch because she wasn't hungry.
They didn't come to class because it was a holiday.
He didn't call because his phone was out of battery.

content.explanation: Use 'but' to connect a positive and negative past action naturally.

examples: I studied but I didn't understand everything.
She came to the office but she didn't stay long.
I didn't go to the gym but I walked for an hour.
He didn't cook but he ordered food.

content.instruction: Repeat naturally. Notice 'didn't' is often spoken quickly as one unit.

phrases.text: I didn't sleep well last night because it was too hot.
She didn't come to work today because she felt sick.
We went to the restaurant but we didn't order dessert.
He didn't know the answer, so he didn't say anything.
I didn't buy anything because everything was too expensive.

content.question: 'I ___ the match because my TV was broken.'
content.explanation: didn't + BASE verb. didn't watch ✓ (not 'watched')

content.question: 'She ___ to the doctor because she felt better.'
content.explanation: Irregular verb 'go' → didn't + BASE verb (go, not went). didn't go ✓

content.question: '___ you eat breakfast this morning?' — 'No, I ___. I wasn't hungry.'
content.explanation: Question: Did you ___? Negative short answer: No, I didn't.

content.question: Choose the most natural sentence:
options: I studied but I didn't understood the topic.
I studied but I didn't understand the topic.
I studied but I don't understand the topic.
I studied but I wasn't understand the topic.
content.explanation: didn't + BASE verb. understand (not understood). I didn't understand ✓

tasks.title: Fill in didn't + correct base verb

content.sentence: Last night I ___ (sleep) well and I ___ (feel) tired this morning. I ___ (want) to get up but I had to.
content.explanation: didn't sleep ✓, felt (positive irregular) ✓, didn't want ✓ (base after didn't).

content.sentence: We ___ to the beach but we ___ in the sea because it was too cold.
content.explanation: went (positive, irregular) + didn't swim (negative, base verb after didn't).

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a correct past negative sentence with a reason.
content.correct_sentence: I didn't go to the party because I was tired.
content.explanation: Subject + didn't + base verb + object + because + reason.

content.instruction: Rearrange to make a sentence contrasting what you did and didn't do.
content.correct_sentence: She cooked but she didn't wash the dishes.
content.explanation: Positive past + but + subject + didn't + base verb + object.

tasks.title: Match the positive to its negative

content.instruction: Match each positive past sentence to its negative version.
This has 3 errors. Choose the correct version.
content.wrong_text: I didn't went to the gym because I didn't felt well. I stayed home but I didn't rested — I worked.
options: I didn't went to the gym because I didn't felt well. I stayed home but I didn't rested — I worked.
I didn't go to the gym because I didn't feel well. I stayed home but I didn't rest — I worked.
I didn't go to the gym because I didn't feel well. I stayed home but I didn't rested — I worked.
I didn't go to the gym because I didn't felt well. I stayed home but I didn't rest — I worked.
content.explanation: didn't go ✓ (not went), didn't feel ✓ (not felt), didn't rest ✓ (not rested) — always base verb after didn't.

tasks.title: Dialogue — I didn't do what I planned!

content.context: Priya planned a productive Sunday but nothing went to plan. She talks to her sister.

dialogue.speaker: Sister
dialogue.line: How was your Sunday? Did you do all the things you planned?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.line: No! I didn't do anything I planned!

dialogue.speaker: Sister
dialogue.line: Really? What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.line: I didn't wake up early — I slept until 11! I planned to go to the gym but I didn't go.

dialogue.speaker: Sister
dialogue.line: What about the cleaning?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.line: I cleaned the kitchen but I didn't clean my room. And I didn't cook — I ordered food.

dialogue.speaker: Sister
dialogue.line: Did you study?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.line: I tried! I opened my book but I didn't read much — I kept watching videos.

dialogue.speaker: Sister
dialogue.line: So what did you actually do?

dialogue.speaker: Priya
dialogue.line: I slept, ate, and watched films. It wasn't productive but it was very relaxing!

dialogue.speaker: Sister
dialogue.line: Sounds like a perfect Sunday to me!

comprehension_questions.question: Did Priya go to the gym?

content.scenario: Your friend asks why you didn't come to their party last week.
content.question: Which is the most natural and grammatically correct reply?
options: I didn't came because I wasn't feeling well and I didn't wanted to make others sick.
I didn't come because I wasn't feeling well and I didn't want to make others sick.
I don't come because I wasn't feel well.
I came not because I was not feeling well.
content.explanation: didn't come ✓, wasn't feeling ✓, didn't want ✓ — all base verbs after didn't.

tasks.title: Speaking — What you planned but didn't do

content.instruction: Speak for 60 seconds. Talk about things you planned to do recently but DIDN'T do. Give reasons.
content.prompt: Tell me 4–5 things you planned to do this week but didn't do. Why not?
content.example_response: This week I planned to wake up early every day but I didn't — I slept late because I was tired. I planned to go to the gym three times but I only went once because work was very busy. I wanted to call my grandmother but I didn't call her — I kept forgetting! I planned to cook healthy food but I didn't cook much — I ordered from outside almost every day. And I planned to finish reading my book but I didn't finish it because I was too distracted by my phone!
key_phrases: I planned to ___ but I didn't ___

content.instruction: Speak for 50 seconds. For each day of the week, say ONE thing you did and ONE thing you didn't do.
content.prompt: Tell me what you did and didn't do on different days this week.
content.example_response: On Monday I went to work but I didn't finish my project. On Tuesday I cooked dinner but I didn't clean up after. On Wednesday I exercised but I didn't eat well. On Thursday I called a friend but I didn't meet them in person. On Friday I went out for dinner but I didn't stay out late. On Saturday I slept in but I didn't waste the whole day — I read for two hours.

content.sentence: Last Saturday I ___ (plan) to clean my house but I ___ (do) it. Instead I ___ (go) to the mall with my friend. We ___ (buy) clothes and ___ (eat) at a nice café. I ___ (come) home late and ___ (not finish) any housework. But I ___ (have) a great time!
content.explanation: planned✓, didn't do✓, went✓, bought✓, ate✓, came✓, didn't finish✓, had✓

what_you_learned: Didn't + BASE verb = past negative for ALL verbs (regular and irregular)
Never: didn't + -ed form or irregular past form
Contrast: I did ___ but I didn't ___
Short answer: Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.
All subjects use same form: I/she/they didn't ___
